description: A lightweight VS Code wrapper for the Arduino App CLI — manage, run and monitor Arduino UNO Q apps, bricks and models.
config.daemon.host: Host where the arduino-app-cli daemon is reachable.
config.daemon.port: TCP port of the arduino-app-cli daemon (the on-board systemd service uses 8800).
config.cliPath: Path to the arduino-app-cli executable, used to run CLI-only commands (clean-cache, system init/cleanup/network-mode/keyboard/set-name).
config.apiKey: Optional API key sent as the X-API-Key header to the daemon.
config.monitor.lineEnding: Line ending appended to text sent in the serial monitor.
config.monitor.autoReconnect: Automatically reconnect the serial monitor when the connection drops.
config.logs.tail: Number of recent log lines to request when opening an app's console.
config.updateNotifications: Show a notification when a system update is available.
config.intellisense.autoConfigure: Regenerate C++ IntelliSense configuration after each successful run.
config.arduinoCliPath: Optional path to a standalone arduino-cli, enabling a fast compilation-database build without flashing.
config.python.manageVenv: Allow the extension to create a Python virtual environment and install requirements for IntelliSense.
config.python.fastReload: Reload a running app's Python on save (instead of a full restart) by injecting a SIGHUP re-exec handler via <app>/bricks/sitecustomize.py and signalling the app's container. Requires VS Code running on the board (e.g. Remote-SSH).
walkthrough.title: Get Started with Arduino App CLI
walkthrough.description: Build, run and monitor Arduino UNO Q apps without leaving VS Code.
walkthrough.connect.title: Connect to your UNO Q
walkthrough.connect.description: This extension talks to the `arduino-app-cli` daemon on your Arduino UNO Q (usually over Remote-SSH). Confirm it's reachable: [Show Version](command:appLab.showVersion).
walkthrough.createApp.title: Create or import an app
walkthrough.createApp.description:
An app bundles a **Python** half (`python/main.py`) and a **C++** sketch (`sketch/sketch.ino`). Start fresh or bring in an existing one.

[New App](command:appLab.newApp) · [Import App](command:appLab.importApp)
walkthrough.bricksModels.title: Add bricks & models
walkthrough.bricksModels.description:
Browse the **Brick Catalog** for reusable components and add them to your app, and import AI **Models** from Edge Impulse.

[Add Brick](command:appLab.app.addBrick)
walkthrough.runApp.title: Run your app
walkthrough.runApp.description:
Press **Run** to compile and flash the sketch to the MCU and start the Python side — progress streams to the **Console**. Stop it any time.

[Run](command:appLab.app.start)
walkthrough.monitor.title: Monitor & plot serial data
walkthrough.monitor.description:
Open the serial **Monitor** to read from the board, or the **Plotter** to chart numeric streams live.

[Open Serial Monitor](command:appLab.openMonitor)
